text:
I know these segments are only in there because parent groups complained. Im gonna be a stick in the mud and say that they were mostly right. It is in fact not good that their kids would watch nothing but violent slop (dont worry, i dont mean your favorite cartoon, because to be your favorite it had to actually survive the filtering) -  you just have no idea how bad some of the programming was at the time - i watch old cartoons for research and a lot of them are very bad on every level, i admit it freely as someone probably your age or older. Before you say "parents should entertain kids instead of a TV" - its unrealistic. 

Parents have their own lives, and so do children. Its completely natural for kids to have their own culture and interests, important in fact that they have self-identification outside of family. I was a teacher, and my mom is a teacher and neurologist specializing in special children. Not wanting to spend time with your family and instead emulating a Blorbo from their tv shows is completely normal. Now its ideal if kids feel safe enough to ask their parents about things theyve seen on TV. But a lot of the times they dont. Too many abused kids confessed they only realized how a good family is structured by watching TV. Imo we cant take risks. And in my own opinion modern cartoons are actually incredible at teaching kids valuable lessons. 

I know this is controversial because "new always equals bad"

 But Im telling you now, modern cartoons are amazing for kids because theyre done by people who care about the medium. It not always used to be like that. People who cared could be counted by the fingers (im basing this off the interviews). A lot of animators and creators hated the cartoons they made and said they only worked there for money.


AOSTH was clearly made with care and love. Though Sonic Sez was mandated they didnt half ass it. These ARE important lessons. I had them taught by my parents, but I knew many kids who didn't have that luxury. Should they be scorned? Should these segments be mocked?

One thign ill say about modern cartoons for kids: I wish they kept those.
author: @KOTEBANAROT

author: @corrygotjokes

text: It was helpful for me back in 5th grade. On the bus ride home, the bus broke down on the train tracks and I was scared if something bad was going to happen and then there was a train coming in the far distance. I started breaking down in tears because I thought I was going to die. Some other kid on the bus had a phone and let me call my dad but he didn’t pick up. That only made things worse. The bus driver walked out of the bus and directed traffic until another bus arrived. Eventually another bus showed up and I got home safely. I told my dad the entire situation and then he told my mom about it. My dad said he had no idea since it was a number he didn’t know. Then for my birthday, they got me my first ever cell phone. I don’t think they were gonna get me a phone until a couple years later but they got it for me because of what happened. Now my dad actually calls back even if it’s a random number. Crazy story that I’ll never forget

Sonic Sez isnt depicting Tails how he is in the canon show, theyre talking about things kids will meet outside. Dark forest could just be a street a kid wandered onto and got scared. text:
Jesus Christ is coming back to Earth very soon! If you want to spend eternity in the Kingdom of Heaven, you must recognize that you are a sinner on your way to Hell (Romans 3:23), and that you are in need of a savior (Romans 6:23). Over 2000 years ago, Jesus lived a perfect life and died on the Cross of Calvery to atone all of your sins (John 3:16). All you have to do to be saved is trust in his shed blood on the Cross of Calvery as your only hope of Heaven (John 6:40). Once you do this, you are sealed by the Holy Spirit until the day of redemption.

John 3:16 - "For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life."

Ephesians 2:8-9 - "For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God: Not of works, lest any man should boast."

John 6:40 - "And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day."

Acts 16:31 - "And they said, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved, and thy house."

Acts 10:43 - "To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins."

John 11:26 - "And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?"

1 Corinthians 15:1-4 - "Moreover, brethren, I declare unto you the gospel which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand; By which also ye are saved, if ye keep in memory what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain. For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures; And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures."                                   ..

text:
Yeah, I mentioned the "in the tree" bit because this video is for the show.

My point still stands, though, that Tails (according to the manuals for Sonic 2 in both Japan and America) was alone and constantly ridiculed because of his mutation until Sonic showed up. So, I think he was abandoned by his real parents because he was a freak of nature.

author: @lilfoxbot

text:
Sonic found Tails abandoned in a tree, so either his parents died or they just dropped him off somewhere because of his mutation.

Though, to be fair, the only two Sonic characters that we know ANYTHING about when it comes to family are Cream and Shadow. Anything could've happened to the parents of all the other characters (though, it's pretty clear that at least Knuckles parents are dead).

And don't factor the comic into this, that only applies to said comic universe.
